primitives.name: MulEvalSound
primitives.purpose: semantic certificate that a coefficient list evaluates as product p*q

primitives.name: mul_eq_zero_or_left_or_right
primitives.purpose: real integral-domain zero-product bridge used by product-root splitting

checked_results.statement: linearCoeff r has degree bound one
linearCoeff r is normalized because its last coefficient is one
coefficient-list linear factor evaluates like the AST linear factor
normalized and AST linear-factor roots are equivalent
a root of a semantically certified product is a root of one factor
if the left factor is nonzero at x, product root implies right root
if the right factor is nonzero at x, product root implies left root

unlocked: linear coefficient-list representation now matches the existing AST linear-factor packet
semantic product certificate gives a safe place to attach future convolution normalizers
product-root splitting is available under an explicit real integral-domain bridge axiom
root-count induction can now target factor/product splitting instead of only base cases
blocked_next: replace bridge axiom with a derived integral-domain theorem if MachLib field substrate expands
implement coefficient-list convolution plus MulEvalSound proofs for concrete products
linear-factor packet for normalized coefficient lists with singleton root list

bridge_axioms.reason: MachLib's current minimal real-field substrate does not yet derive zero-product splitting.
bridge_axioms.replacement_path: derive from expanded field/integral-domain substrate or add a verified domain layer
